The '''carrion spat''' was an flightless [[Bird/Legends|avian]] creature native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].

==Biology and appearance==
It had common ratite features: flightless and large, with a raptor-like beak and two long legs. Like the [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], carrion spats had no upper extremities at all, unusual among much of galactic fauna. They were mostly featherless, but both [[Gender/Legends|males]] and females alike had plumage issuing from their upper pelvic regions.

[[File:Oneu_Bliss3_SWG_Coronet.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A giant carrion spat standing beside its handler in [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet]].]]
Plumage as well as coloration varied among carrion spats, ranging from [[Color/Legends|crimson]] and brown to shades of green. They were often domesticated, and could be fitted with a harness and bridle, serving as popular mounts for [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species throughout the galaxy. Some could be found on [[Boz Pity/Legends|Boz Pity]].<ref name="renegade">''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]''</ref> Like the [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]], the carrion spat sometimes served as a military mount for various groups because of its excellent speed and mobility over terrain.

Carrion spats could be domesticated and used as mounts for transportation.<ref name="SWG" />

===Variants===
A larger, variant subspecies called the ''"giant carrion spat"'' existed as well on [[Corellia]]. This subspecies, apart from size, was very similar in appearance to the common carrion spat. Aside from physical appearance, the giant carrion spat was much less common and could be observed, in the wild, only at very high elevations.

The [[painted spat]] was a larger, stronger, more resistant and more aggressive mutated variant of carrion spat.<ref name="SWG:TTE" />

==History==
{{Quote|Well the first problem has to do with some enraged carrion spats. I don't rightly know what enraged them, but they do a lot of damage. I need you to go thin out their pack and defeat their pack leader, Gravefeather.|[[Pol Handerin]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:HFPH">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Help Farmer Pol Handerin|Corellia}}</ref>}}
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], a pack of carrion spats lived in the [[Riverlands]] of Corellia.<ref name="SWG:HFPH" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the enraged carrion spats attacked the [[Farm/Legends|farms]] on the outskirts of [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]], such as [[Pol Handerin]]'s [[Pol Handerin's farm|farm]]. [[Corellian Security Forces|CorSec]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Mack Jasper]] dispatched a recruit to kill the pack leader [[Gravefeather]] and most of the pack.<ref name="SWG:HFPH" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Mining/Legends|miner]] [[Volo Madine]] owned carrion spat named [[Betsy]].<ref name="SWG:FBO">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Find Betsy's Owner|Dathomir}}</ref> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline" /> Madine and Betsy were present on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] during the [[Imperial bioweapons Project I71A|Blackwing virus]] [[Blackwing virus outbreak on Dathomir|outbreak]]. The miner barely escaped the [[Zombie/Legends|undeads]] who attacked him but he lost his carrion spat in his run. Later, a spacer retrieved Betsy alive in the [[Quarantine Zone (Dathomir)|Quarantine Zone]] and brought it back to her owner.<ref name="SWG:FBO" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ostrich_concept_art.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Concept art for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.]]
Carrion spats appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a massively multiplayer online role-playing game developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Carrion spats were made craftable mounts for players with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 4 Notes: 13 November 03|Publish 4]]" on [[November 13]], 2003.<ref name="SonyCite-Publish 4 Mounts and Cities">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=60891|text=Publish 4 Mounts and Cities}}</ref> This feature was removed from the game upon the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> However, it was finally restored with the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 6 Notes for May 22 2007|Chapter 6]]" update, released on [[May 22]], [[2007]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Notes – Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67250&month=052007|text=Publish Notes – Chapter 6: Masters of the Wild}}</ref>
